entry.note: Hash-chained append-only log starts here. Preceding 3 entries are pre-chain legacy prologue (accepted per RESEARCH_RECORD_INTEGRITY.md). All subsequent entries must be hash-chained.

entry.rationale: 52-week-high disposition effect documented in US equities. Tier-1 factor in plan.
Momentum DEAD on TWSE; plan hypothesizes may WORK on NYSE. No directional prior.

entry.prediction: OOS Sharpe in [0.5, 0.8] on research period
entry.rationale: Phase 3 exit target per plan Build Phases

entry.prediction: OOS Sharpe > 0 required on one-shot holdout
entry.rationale: Statistical Validation Suite step 8; any positive admits paper, negative STOPS

entry.detail: See docs/INDEPENDENT_VALIDATION_DRAFT.md section 4, docs/OUTCOME_VS_FORECAST.md row factor-ivol_20d-2016_2023. Raw IC pre-2020 = +0.0213 (directional signal present). 2016-2023 low-vol winter + Q1 2021 meme squeeze are leading hypotheses. AP-6 upheld: sign NOT flipped.

entry.summary: Scripts/screen_factor.py wrote two raw (non-chained) log entries before being patched to use append_event(). A subsequent append via this script read the last raw line, found no hash field, returned genesis, and started a fork chain with 2 entries. Incident was caught by verify_research_log.py within minutes and repaired same-day.
actions_taken: Truncated log to last known-good hash d047beee
Fixed _last_hash() in append_research_log.py to scan for last line carrying a hash field, ignoring any trailing legacy entries
Fixed screen_factor.py to call append_event() rather than writing raw jsonl
Re-appending the two factor_screen events via the corrected tooling
entry.note: NO research findings were altered. Only transport of those findings into the chain was rebuilt. Backed-up file retains forensic evidence of the original break.

entry.motivation: Test leading hypothesis #1 (low-vol winter 2016-2019) from ivol_20d MISS notes
entry.methodology: Weekly Spearman IC between ranked(-ivol) and 5d fwd ret, stratified by pre/post-2020, SMA-200 bull/bear on cap-weighted market proxy, and year
findings: Low-vol-winter hypothesis partially supported (2019-2021 positive) but 2016-2018 strongly negative contradicts single-regime framing
Pre-2020 vs post-2020 difference is negligible (~0.0016 IC)
Regime (SMA-200 bull/bear) is strongest axis: bull IC near zero, bear IC = -0.034
Year-over-year dispersion is wide: 4pp IC swing between best (2020) and worst (2023)
entry.decision: Evidence archived. Construct/reject decision on TODO-23 regime-conditional variant deferred until fundamental factors (piotroski, earnings_surprise, accruals, profitability) complete gate evaluation
entry.ap6_note: No sign change made to ivol_20d compute fn. Any regime-conditional variant must be pre-registered as a fresh forecast before screening

entry.motivation: Close enterprise-tier documentation gap between research-only artifacts and LP/DDQ-ready package. Pre-first-LP-engagement. Six gaps identified during /sparc:documenter audit of high-quality doc standards: no ODD package, no quarterly-letter template, no stress-test methodology, no attribution methodology, no consolidated risk-limits document, no one-shot reproducibility driver.
gaps_closed: ODD (operational diligence) — separate from investment DDQ; covers entity, vendors, cyber, BCP
Quarterly letter — template enforcing traceability, RSP benchmark, Brinson attribution
Stress-test framework — 6 named historical scenarios + bootstrap + synthetic shocks
Attribution methodology — Brinson sector + factor-contribution formulas + invariant tests
Risk limits — consolidated single-source-of-truth across 5 layers + gaps section
Reproducibility driver — scripts/reproduce.sh with manifest pairing every number to artifact
artifacts_modified: docs/DDQ_AIMA_2025.md (§15.4 expanded with high_52w + momentum_2_12 outcomes; §15.4a regime investigation; §15.6 reproducibility; Appendix A extended; v0.2 change log entry)
entry.ap6_check: pass — no factor thresholds, gate thresholds, or trigger thresholds changed. Documentation additions only. The three completed factor screens (ivol_20d FAIL, high_52w FAIL, momentum_2_12 4/6) remain as recorded on 2026-04-18 without re-screening or re-tuning.
non_coverage: ODD §3 service providers remain TBD-LIVE pending vendor contract execution
QUARTERLY_LETTER_TEMPLATE is a template, not a filled letter — no live period to report
STRESS_TEST_FRAMEWORK lists scenarios but no scenario replay has been run; expected behaviors are design-derived
ATTRIBUTION_METHODOLOGY.md has no worked example with numbers (AP-6: avoid stale demo numbers being misread)
RISK_LIMITS.md gaps section (§6) lists 6 limits that are not yet codified (net exposure, currency, factor crowding, correlation cap, consecutive loss, leverage)
reproduce.sh does not cover holdout (2024-2025), paper trading, live fills, or EDGAR/FINRA-sourced factors
entry.skill_context: /sparc:documenter high-quality-doc audit followed by /document-release gap-closure workflow

entry.interpretation: Statistically distinguishable from noise (p=0.002) but cross-sectional signal is economically weak: IC ~0.009 is <half the 0.02 admission threshold and IC-IR 0.089 is ~6x below 0.5 gate. Long-short portfolio Sharpe 0.04 confirms weak economic magnitude. Factor rejected.
entry.ap6_note: First screening of piotroski on real EDGAR companyfacts data. No prior screenings to be amended.

entry.interpretation: Long-short portfolio Sharpe 0.58 clears G0, signal statistically non-null (p=0.002), but cross-sectional IC 0.008 and IC-IR 0.062 fail by wide margin. Pattern mirrors piotroski: real information in ordering, but magnitude too small to overcome transaction costs at the factor level. Factor rejected.
entry.ap6_note: First screening of accruals (Collins-Hribar style, OANCF - NI excess proxy). No prior screenings to be amended.

entry.interpretation: Strongest of the three fundamental factors: long-short Sharpe 1.15 far exceeds G0, max_dd -19% well inside G4, statistically significant (p=0.002). Cross-sectional IC 0.016 still below 0.02 threshold and IC-IR 0.113 at ~22% of G3 requirement. Portfolio-level result compelling but per-name ranking signal remains weak by admission standards. Factor rejected under current gate thresholds.
entry.ap6_note: First screening of profitability (Novy-Marx gross-profits-to-assets). G3 IC-IR gate of 0.5 is materially stricter than the plan.md proposal of 0.02; discrepancy documented but NOT adjusted post-hoc per AP-6.

entry.summary: Landed machine-readable research-pack contract. `scripts/make_research_pack.py` (320 LOC) emits `results/packs/<run_id>/manifest.json` plus snapshot copies of `config/*`, `uv.lock`, and `pyproject.toml`. Manifest shape is enforced: run_id, run_label, generated_at, git (sha/short/branch/dirty/dirty_files_count), python, platform, dependencies (pyproject_sha256/uv_lock_sha256/uv_lock_present), configs (filename->sha256), data_snapshot (path/schema_hash/table_row_counts or null), research_log_tip (hash/height or null), reproduction (env_setup_cmds/verify_chain_cmd/rerun_command), manifest_sha256 (self-hash over canonical JSON excluding itself). DuckDB fingerprint is schema+row-count hash (stable across re-opens), not raw page bytes. 11 integration tests use real DuckDB + real hash-chained log per iron rule 3 (no mocks on database path). `docs/REPRODUCIBILITY.md` documents generation, manifest shape, re-verification protocol (5 checks: dependency drift, config drift, schema drift, chain continuity, self-integrity), and end-to-end factor-screen rerun recipe.

manifest_contract.self_hash_method: sha256 over canonical json(sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':')) of manifest with manifest_sha256 omitted
manifest_contract.data_snapshot_fingerprint: sha256 over canonical json of {tables:[{table,columns:[[col_name,data_type],...]}], counts:{table->row_count}}
manifest_contract.tamper_evident_anchor: caller appends a research_log event whose payload embeds manifest_sha256

iron_rule_compliance.rule_1_no_post_2023_dates: script defaults db-path to research.duckdb (pre-2024) and docs explicitly forbid pointing at 2024+ data outside the one-shot holdout evaluation
iron_rule_compliance.rule_3_no_db_mocks: 11 integration tests build a real tmp_path DuckDB via duckdb.connect + CREATE TABLE + INSERT, plus a real hash-chained research_log.jsonl fixture; zero mocks on the database path
iron_rule_compliance.rule_6_hash_chain_contract_preserved: script never touches results/research_log.jsonl; anchoring happens via scripts/append_research_log.py so chain continuity is unaffected

entry.summary: Landed per-vendor due-diligence files under docs/vendors/ (FINMIND.md 110 LOC, EDGAR.md 106 LOC, FINRA.md 111 LOC). Each file uses a shared 9-section template: (1) purpose & pipeline usage with downstream factor list and outage blast radius, (2) endpoint + contact + escalation path, (3) auth method + rate limits with adapter file:line cites, (4) license/ToS summary, (5) PiT publication lag with holdout guard cross-ref, (6) failover plan, (7) known data-quality issues table, (8) append-only historical outage log seeded empty, (9) review cadence + ownership. EDGAR file documents the User-Agent-only auth and 10 req/s SEC fair-access limit with the 403-on-missing-UA response. entry.summary: Created docs/GOVERNANCE_LOG.md (190 LOC) as the append-only authorization register per SR 11-7 §VII. Distinct from docs/AUDIT_TRAIL.md (what ran) — this records what was sanctioned. Schema columns: decision_id, date, decision, approver(s), criteria cited, evidence, dissent. Seeded §4 with 9 authorization rows: GL-0001 falsification-trigger freeze 2026-04-15 (config/falsification_triggers.yaml:5); GL-0002..GL-0007 the 6 G0-G5 factor rejections (ivol_20d, high_52w, momentum_2_12, piotroski, accruals, profitability) each citing results/factors/<name>/gate_results.json + research-log line; GL-0003 the regime-conditional IVOL DEFER decision citing investigation hash cfbf5e618e85...; GL-0009 the 6-of-6 failure-state acknowledgement citing commit 588ffce. §5 lists 13 pending authorization points with frozen criteria (stage-gate graduations paper→shadow→minimum_live→scale from config/deployment_ladder.yaml, VETO F1-F3 and WARNING F4-F8 responses from config/falsification_triggers.yaml, threshold-change gate, model-swap gate, kill-switch activation). §2 change protocol mandates: append-only, evidence required, approver named, dissent recorded even if 'none', criteria must already exist in a frozen artifact (AP-6), commit SHA reference required. §6 reversal protocol requires a new row that cross-references the reversed decision_id — no editing of history. §8 cross-references pull in AUDIT_TRAIL, RISK_REGISTER, MODEL_VALIDATION §1.5 (partial independence), SEC_FINRA_COMPLIANCE, RALPH_LOOP_TASK iron rules, research_log.jsonl, deployment_ladder.yaml, falsification_triggers.yaml. P2 block progress: 6/9 closed (14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19); 3 remaining (20 compliance attestations, 21 capacity placeholders blocked on TODO-11 per iron rule 7, 22 plain-English summary).

entry.summary: Landed two daily-attestation templates operationalizing SEC Rule 15c3-5 (Market Access pre-order risk checks) and FINRA Rule 3110 (supervisory review evidence). docs/templates/PRE_TRADE_ATTESTATION.md (14 gated checks over 8 sections) is completed before the first order of the day — §2 kill-switch + VETO state (kill_switch flag at config/strategy_params.yaml:38, outstanding VETO via live.duckdb.falsification_checks last row, frozen-hash match via src/nyse_ats/monitoring/falsification.py:50-82 verify_frozen_hash); §3 risk limits (max_position_pct ≤ 0.10, max_sector_pct ≤ 0.30, beta ∈ [0.5, 1.5], daily_loss_limit = -0.03, earnings_event_cap) citing config/strategy_params.yaml:29-36; §4 data + CA (F8 staleness ≤ 10d, corporate-action guard via src/nyse_ats/execution/nautilus_bridge.py:99-157 pre_submit, universe PiT); §5 iron-rule compliance (no post-2023 timestamps at research stage, research-log chain verifies, stage-gate preconditions satisfied); §6 proposed TradePlan envelope; §7 operator sign-off with commit SHA as signature; §8 exception protocol requiring a GL-NNNN reference to GOVERNANCE_LOG. docs/templates/POST_TRADE_ATTESTATION.md (19 gated checks over 11 sections) is completed after EOD reconciliation — §2 fills-vs-plan (fill_rate ≥ 95%, rejection_rate < 5%, mean_slippage < 20 bps, settlement_failures = 0 per config/deployment_ladder.yaml:40 graduation gates); §3 rejection detail; §4 daily P&L vs daily_loss_limit (-3%) and drawdown vs F3 VETO (-25%); §5 realized concentration; §6 F1-F8 trigger status; §7 cumulative cost drag vs F6 (5%) and turnover vs F5 (200%); §8 corporate actions in window; §9 iron-rule post-trade compliance; §10 operator sign-off; §11 exception protocol requiring BOTH GL-NNNN and AT-NNNN references. Both templates frozen 2026-04-19 — changes require a row in GOVERNANCE_LOG.md under the threshold-change authorization point (AP-6). Added §7 to docs/SEC_FINRA_COMPLIANCE.md linking both templates with 6-year retention protocol and the dual GL-NNNN + AT-NNNN override-reference requirement. P2 block progress: 7/9 closed (14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20); 2 remaining (21 capacity placeholders blocked on TODO-11 per iron rule 7, 22 plain-English summary).

entry.summary: Created docs/EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_NONQUANT.md (115 LOC) as the CRO/CCO/audit-facing companion to docs/NYSE_ALPHA_ONE_PAGER.md (which is pitched to quant/investor audience). Completion criterion 9 explicitly names this file as required. Structure per TODO-22 spec: (1) What we do — 1 paragraph establishing systematic long-only S&P-500 strategy with weekly Friday rebalance and Monday execution, plus explicit 'not trading any money today' caveat; (2) Who benefits — 1 paragraph framing clients / firm / auditors / regulators as beneficiaries with plain-English reasoning; (3) How we control risk — 5 bullets covering pre-declared stop rules (F1-F8 frozen 2026-04-15), daily signed attestations with 6-year retention, position/sector/beta/daily-loss limits cited against config/strategy_params.yaml, untouched 2024-2025 holdout, append-only GOVERNANCE_LOG.md authorization register; (4) What would cause us to halt — 5 bullets covering F1/F2 signal-and-factor death VETO, F3 drawdown VETO (-25%), daily_loss_limit intraday kill-switch (-3%), F8 data-staleness / corporate-action halt, iron-rule violation halt; (5) How we prove it works — 3 bullets covering pre-registered OUTCOME_VS_FORECAST tracking, reproducibility from raw vendor pulls + research-log hash chain, honest 6-of-6 failure reporting with GL-0009 cross-reference; (6) Ownership table — 8 roles (PM / CRO / CCO / Model Validator / CTO / Internal Audit / External Counsel / Investment Committee) each mapped to 1-3 primary artifact paths. Current-state block names: stage=research, 0 factors admitted, 6 rejected (GL-0002..GL-0007), stop thresholds frozen 2026-04-15 (GL-0001), holdout untouched, 9 authorization rows, attestations not yet in daily use because no trading. 'Who to contact' routes questions by domain. Explicitly NO IC / IC_IR / Romano-Wolf / permutation / bootstrap / Ridge / purged-CV jargon. Flagged in preamble that it and docs/NYSE_ALPHA_ONE_PAGER.md MUST stay consistent — change-protocol requirement. P2 block progress: 8/9 closed (14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 22); 1 remaining (21 capacity placeholders blocked on TODO-11 per iron rule 7).

entry.summary: Closed RALPH completion criterion 8 (docs/FRAMEWORK_AND_PIPELINE.pdf regenerated today via scripts/regen_framework_pdf.sh). Created scripts/regen_framework_pdf.sh as an executable bash script (chmod 755) that invokes md-to-pdf v5.2.5 on docs/FRAMEWORK_AND_PIPELINE.md using --config-file config/puppeteer.config.js. The config file (committed separately in this same iteration) carries Puppeteer launch_options.args = [--no-sandbox, --disable-setuid-sandbox, --disable-dev-shm-usage] plus pdf_options (A4, 20mm/18mm margins, printBackground=true). The two-file split enforces single source of truth for Chromium flags (iron-rule adjacent: config over CLI). Script prints input path, md-to-pdf version, config path, output path, byte count, and SHA-256 for audit traceability. On this run: output docs/FRAMEWORK_AND_PIPELINE.pdf = 1,787,534 bytes, sha256 = a13b0cb8302ab6f8d73f9597ed1cc8d029e1f087f2c5fb74c113f95e5e585295. Script uses set -euo pipefail and exits non-zero if input md missing, config missing, md-to-pdf not on PATH, or output PDF not produced — no silent failures (iron rule 5). Does not pass --launch-options on CLI (would drift from config/puppeteer.config.js). Remaining blockers to completion promise: criterion 2 / TODO-21 (capacity placeholders) remains OPEN, blocked by iron rule 7 (cannot touch TODO-11 real-data factor-screening work in this loop). Completion promise NOT emitted.

entry.summary: Closed RALPH-internal TODO-20 (docs/RALPH_LOOP_TASK.md line 36). Created docs/templates/factor_screen_memo.md (244 LOC) as the frozen single-layout template for every factor screen memo from 2026-04-19 forward. Sections per RALPH spec: hypothesis, data window, G0-G5 verdict, permutation p, Romano-Wolf adjusted p, bootstrap CI, decision; plus header/metadata with pre-screen and post-screen research-log anchors, holdout-untouched attestation, three-layer statistical significance rule (raw permutation + Romano-Wolf + bootstrap CI lower bound > 0 — all three must agree), decision ADMIT/REJECT/HOLD (exactly one; rejection memos cannot propose rescue variants — that is a separate pre-registered screen), attestations + commit-SHA-as-signature table, file-pointer registry, change-protocol AP-6 freeze. Template bans forecast backfilling: 'If this table is filled after the screen ran, the memo is invalid. The forecast must chain to the research log before the screen completes.' All gate thresholds cited read-only against config/gates.yaml. Six factors screened piecemeal before the template existed; every factor screen from today onward uses this frozen layout. Completion promise remains NOT EMITTED — docs/TODOS.md TODO-21 (capacity placeholders) stays blocked on TODO-11 per iron rule 7, so criterion 2 cannot fully close in this session.
